_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Produce digital network intelligence against specific named target sets.
reference_document_element_description: Establish, communicate, and enforce enterprise-wide data and AI policies covering quality, privacy, security, ethics, and responsible use.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-T-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-K-005 (RM, cosine: 0.410), SP-T-010 (SP, cosine: 0.409).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 2.0/10 (raw 0.205). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [against, digital, intelligence, named, network, produce] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, communicate, covering, data, enforce, enterprise] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.839 (P=0.853, R=0.825). P exceeds R by 0.028 — the DCWF element's 9 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 17 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (98.7%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Produce digital network intelligence against specific named target sets." and "Establish, communicate, and enforce enterprise-wide data and AI policies coverin". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.419 > STS 0.205 (gap +0.214). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [against, digital, intelligence, named, network, produce]. WIDAI terms: [ai, communicate, covering, data, enforce, enterprise].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 98.7% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.214 with low STS 0.205. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
